pending.body:
finding_summary: "`sync_after_close` can silently skip the required crash-recovery flush because it checks `has_pending_mutations()` before calling `sync_if_dirty()`. `has_pending_mutations()` is only the adapter's cached flag, while `sync_if_dirty()` explicitly re-reads `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations*` under the repo lock (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1233-1263`). If pending state appears after the adapter was constructed, success reconciliation returns from `sync_after_close` without flushing and can advance milestone/controller state while the bead close is still only local."
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:461
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:467
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1233
remediation_hint: "Do not gate this path on `has_pending_mutations()`. Always call `sync_if_dirty()` here and branch on `SyncIfDirtyOutcome` so the repo-state recheck stays authoritative."

finding_summary: "`sync_recovered_proposed_bead_replay_if_dirty` only performs the pre-flush safety check when `has_pending_mutations()` is already set. That flag is just a cached snapshot; `sync_if_dirty()` may still discover newly persisted pending mutations after adapter construction. In that case this helper will flush them without first rejecting conflicted/missing `.beads/issues.jsonl`, which defeats the new 'block unsafe bead mutations before replay' rule."
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3014
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3020
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1233
remediation_hint: "Make the health gate authoritative at flush time: either run the health check unconditionally before `sync_if_dirty()`, or add a `BrMutationAdapter` helper that combines 're-read repo pending state' with the health check under the same lock."

status_summary: running: final review restart round 17 -> Plan and Implement
